slides.lead: This is a demo (sample) presentation of the template. All text is dummy and only serves to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event title, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Tools, workflows and the biggest bottlenecks today.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Plugins and presets are everywhere, but value comes from taste and intent, not from another tool.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: saved weekly with a reusable mixing template
more finished tracks after tightening the workflow
bullets: Measure outcomes, not plugin count: a finished song wins.
Start where you already have stems and the most friction.
A small, well-mixed demo beats a huge unfinished session.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: Structure first, ear second, taste throughout.
bullets: Stems, tempo map and a clear reference track.
Levels and panning before any effect.
EQ and compression to carve space.
Bus processing to bind the whole mix.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same song, two workflows and two cost curves.
cards: Everything inside the DAW. Recallable and cheap.
Analog hardware for character. Rich but slower.
Digital routing into a few key analog units.
A locked starting session you reuse every time.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a modern audio chain.
cards: The hub where tracking and mixing happen.
Carve frequencies so each part has its place.
Control dynamics and add punch or glue.
Place sounds in a believable space.
Sessions and stems safe in versioned storage.
Share rough mixes and gather clear feedback.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Output stabilizes, polishing the back catalog pays off.
One track breaks out and reshapes the whole plan.
AI assists arrangement, not just routine editing.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: The best gear in the world will not save a song that was not worth recording.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Compare every mix against a track you trust.
Fresh ears catch what tired ears miss.
Print decisions so the session stays light.
slides.notes: A plain bulleted slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A session without a reference and a deadline never ships.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the DAW and balance a rough mix live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
